manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: loftiness, narrowness, backbreaker, candlepower, demineralization, nearness, defacement, denaturalization, checkmark.
Your opponent's team words are: typeset, demobilization, emersion, darer, nightcaps, hotelkeeper, dualism, shortlist.
Distractor words are: pleasantness, denseness, assassinator, tonsillitis, defenseman, fortunetelling, secession.
The kill words are: shirtfront.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of the team's words. Need single-word clue not using any words from list or parts. Must be semantically related. Let's look at our team words: loftiness, narrowness, backbreaker, candlepower, demineralization, nearness, defacement, denaturalization, checkmark.

We can try clue "distance" maybe relates to nearness (opposite) not good. "Light" relates to candlepower. "Strength" relates to backbreaker. "Purity" maybe demineralization, denaturalization. "Sharp" maybe checkmark? "Precision". Could we try clue that relates to multiple? Maybe "measurement": narrowness, nearness, loftiness (height), checkmark (maybe not). "Scale"? hmm.

We need to avoid opponent words: typeset, demobilization, emersion, darer, nightcaps, hotelkeeper, dualism, shortlist. Also avoid distractors: pleasantness, denseness, assassinator, tonsillitis, defenseman, fortunetelling, secession. Also can't include parts of any. So clue must be safe word not containing any of those substrings.

Maybe clue "geometry": relates to narrowness (width), loftiness (height), nearness (distance). But checkmark? maybe not. Could relate to backbreaker? not geometry.

Could target 3 words: candlepower (light), backbreaker (strength), checkmark (validation). Clue "approval"? checkmark only.

Maybe clue "acid": demineralization (removing minerals), denaturalization (removing nature), defacement (damage), but acid also narrowness? Not.

Clue "mineral": demineralization is opposite. Might be confusing.

We can choose to target a single word to be safe. Provide clue "lumens" for candlepower. Is "lumens" part of any other word? No. It's not in lists. Good.

But we need to ensure not part of opponent or distractors. "lumens" contains "lum" not in any list.

Thus:

CLUE: lumens
TARGETS: candlepower

Check if "lumens" is semantically related to candlepower—yes both measure light. Good.

Return format
